channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: NGO Urges Swift Justice for Convicted Child Abuser in South Africa
item.description: An NGO focused on combating child abuse has called for the immediate sentencing of Iain Ware, an 86-year-old convicted sex offender, following the postponement of his pre-sentencing trial in the Wynbe...

item.title: South Air Launches Inaugural Flights from Karachi to Quetta and Islamabad, Enhancing Domestic Connectivity
item.description: South Air, a new private airline, has commenced its inaugural flights from Karachi to Quetta via Turbat and to Islamabad via Bahawalpur, as confirmed by the Pakistan Airports Authority (PAA). The PAA ...

item.title: Aina secures $5.5M to develop AI control devices beyond simple recording
item.description: Aina, a startup founded by Apoorv Shankar—a former Ultrahuman hardware VP—has raised $5.5 million in funding led by Redstart Labs and 360 ONE. The investment comes as the company aims to create hardwa...

item.title: New York Times Focus Group Finds Some Young Trump Voters Dissatisfied With Economy and Foreign Policy
item.description: The article reports on a New York Times focus group involving 10 voters between the ages of 20 and 25, most of whom supported Donald Trump in the 2024 presidential election. While the participants wer...

item.title: Alego Usonga MP Samuel Atandi Donates KSh 500k to Tony Ndiema's Burial
item.description: MP Samuel Atandi, representing Alego Usonga, donated KSh 500,000 towards the burial of late Ohangla singer Tony Ndiema during a candlelight vigil held on July 15, 2026. The event took place at a club ...

item.title: Happy Farm in Tarlac: A Family-Friendly Tropical Retreat
item.description: The article highlights Happy Farm in the Tropics, a resort located in Santa Ignacia, Tarlac, designed as a family-friendly destination. It emphasizes the farm's vibrant atmosphere, ideal for families ...

item.title: Fatal bus crash on Abuja-Kaduna highway claims 11 lives
item.description: A commercial bus carrying 16 passengers collided with a stationary truck on the Abuja-Kaduna highway, killing 11 people and injuring five others. The Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC) confirmed the inc...

item.title: Canada Considers Permanent Daylight Saving Time After U.S. Bill Passes
item.description: The U.S. House of Representatives passed a bill to end seasonal time changes, sparking debate about Canada's potential follow suit. While provinces like Saskatchewan, Alberta, and British Columbia hav...

item.title: Arizona County Reaches Election Management Agreement Amid Political Tensions
item.description: Election officials in Arizona's most populous county, Maricopa, have reached a settlement to resolve a months-long legal battle over election oversight. Republican county recorder Justin Heap and the ...

item.title: Argentina's dramatic comeback secures World Cup final spot after late goals
item.description: In a thrilling World Cup semi-final, Argentina staged a stunning late comeback to defeat England and secure their place in the final. The defending champions trailed 1-0 in the 84th minute but respond...

item.title: Iran's Strategic Advantage Through the Strait of Hormuz and Ceasefire Tensions
item.description: The article examines Iran's strategic leverage over the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for global oil exports, following the collapse of a ceasefire agreement. Iranian Foreign Affairs Ministe...

item.title: Adolphe Muzito Advocates for Extended Presidency of Félix Tshisekedi Due to Economic Recovery
item.description: Adolphe Muzito, a former opposition leader turned budget minister, argues that economic growth in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) has restored the country's financial independence, potentially ...

item.title: Rep. Thomas Massie Aligns with Melania Trump on Epstein Case
item.description: Rep. Thomas Massie (R-KY), a vocal critic of President Donald Trump, has publicly aligned with First Lady Melania Trump's assertion that Jeffrey Epstein did not act alone in his criminal activities. I...

item.title: Ramaphosa Faces Legal Challenge Over Impeachment Proceedings
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa's legal battle against parliamentary impeachment proceedings has entered a critical phase, with the Western Cape High Court set to deliver its judgment on an urgent applicati...

item.title: Alberta's oil production expansion faces challenges in demand, labor, and emissions amid carbon capture plans
item.description: The article discusses Alberta's ambitious plan to expand oilsands production while advancing a $16-billion carbon capture project. The province has agreed to implement financial supports to encourage ...

item.title: US and Chinese Foreign Ministers Attend ASEAN Meetings in Metro Manila Next Week
item.description: The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) confirmed that US Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi will attend the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) and related...

item.title: Tanzania aims to expand geothermal energy capacity to 130MW by 2030
item.description: Tanzania is targeting a contribution of up to 130 megawatts (MW) of geothermal electricity by 2030 as part of efforts to diversify its energy mix and enhance power supply reliability. This goal aligns...

item.title: Celebrity Barber Sam Kitiki Battles GIST Cancer and Financial Struggles
item.description: Sam Kitiki, a well-known Kenyan celebrity barber, has shared a heartfelt video revealing his ongoing battle with GIST cancer and the financial challenges he faces. The illness has left him visibly fra...

item.title: CAC enforces compliance by removing non-compliant companies
item.description: The Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) has started removing 100,000 companies from Nigeria's corporate register as part of its effort to enforce compliance with the Companies and Allied Matters Act (C...

item.title: MI5 provided false evidence to courts in neo-Nazi spy case
item.description: A damning report by Sir John Goldring reveals that MI5 gave false evidence to three courts regarding its 'neither confirm nor deny' (NCND) policy towards a violent neo-Nazi informant, Agent X. The inv...
